methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $42M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Atomic AI is a Series A biotech company that has raised $42M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comparables universe of 8 private companies at Series B stage within Biotech & Health, supplemented by 3 public sector peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L). The peer set includes Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M), establishing a median private valuation of $805M with a range of $100M to $3.3B.

Two valuation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $42M total raised as a value proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, implies an $84M valuation. Peer-median triangulation across the 6 identified same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields $805M. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 5.8x across the 3 public tickers) could not be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, standard for private companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $71M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $444M. The wide dispersion reflects uncertainty around technology maturity relative to the peer set—particularly whether Atomic AI's development trajectory aligns with Series B-stage companies or remains earlier in execution risk. Key variables include near-term financing readiness and whether subsequent fundraising will be priced closer to the stage-progression floor or the peer-median benchmark.
